title: US CPI Forecast Snapshot — July 2026 (v3.1 EIA-Validated Methodology)
methodology: Two-stage aggregation using exact BLS 2024-2025 relative importance weights. Stage 1: component-level forecasts. Stage 2: weighted aggregation. Verification: Food+Energy+Core=100.000% and Shelter+CoreGoods+CoreServices=80.557%. MEASURED MONTH = JULY 2026 (released Aug 12, 2026). July is in progress, so inputs are PROVISIONAL through the run timestamp. Energy validated with AAA retail gasoline ($3.872/gal Jul 13, rising in July). WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R2=0.23, MAE=8.1%). Cross-check: Cleveland Fed CPI Nowcast projects July headline 3.71% y/y and core 2.81% y/y; Kalshi market-implied July headline ~3.6-3.7% y/y. Backtest: 15 months weight accuracy MAE 0.035% core, 0.146% headline.
summary: JULY 2026 CPI (released Aug 12, 08:30 ET). Forecast: headline +0.2% m/m (3.7% y/y; Cleveland nowcast 3.71%; Kalshi ~3.6-3.7%); core +0.2% m/m (2.8% y/y; nowcast 2.81%). The swing factor is energy: the Hormuz escalation is a JULY event (Jul 7 vessel attacks, Jul 8 US strikes + ceasefire declared over + sanctions waiver revoked, Jul 13 blockade reinstated), so July energy CPI turns back UP after June's deflation. Core keeps cooling gradually (shelter easing, services sticky). July is only ~2 weeks in, so this is PROVISIONAL with LOW confidence — the rest of July's oil path decides the print. 10Y 4.57%, VIX ~17, S&P 500 7508. FOMC Jul 28-29 lands before this release.
baseCase: July energy re-accelerates on the Hormuz escalation while core keeps cooling gradually. Headline firms to ~+0.2% m/m / 3.7% y/y; core holds ~+0.2% / 2.8%. Upside risk on headline from oil.

aggregation.verification: Food(13.25) + Energy(6.193) + Core(80.557) = 100.000% | Shelter(34.411) + CoreGoods(18.4) + CoreServices(27.746) = 80.557% | July inputs PROVISIONAL

release.name: CPI
release.thesis: JULY 2026 print (Aug 12). Energy is the driver: the Hormuz escalation is a July event — Jul 7 vessel attacks, Jul 8 US strikes + ceasefire declared over + Treasury sanctions waiver revoked, Jul 13 blockade reinstated (WTI ~$79, gasoline futures +6%). July energy CPI turns back UP after June's deflation, firming headline to ~+0.2% m/m / 3.7% y/y. Core keeps cooling to ~+0.2% / 2.8% y/y (shelter easing, services sticky). Cleveland nowcast: headline 3.71%, core 2.81% y/y. PROVISIONAL — only ~2 weeks of July data; the back-half oil path sets the final number. Prior print = May actual (June actual releases today Jul 14, becomes new prior).

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.2% m/m, Core +0.2% m/m — July energy re-accelerates modestly on the Hormuz escalation; core cools gradually, shelter easing offsets sticky services.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y holds 4.55-4.65%, DXY ~100-101. Choppy tape: $XLE and $GLD bid on the energy/geopolitical tail, equities range-bound into FOMC (Jul 28-29). $QQQ capped by higher-for-longer; $TLT tentative. Headline firm but not a shock — market reads it as transitory energy, not core re-acceleration.
scenarios.invalidation: Surprise Hormuz de-escalation/reopening collapses oil back below $70, or core services break decisively lower.

scenarios.label: Hotter inflation surprise
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.4-0.5% m/m, Core +0.3% m/m — Hormuz escalation sustains the oil spike through July, gasoline rises sharply, services stay sticky; y/y back toward 4%.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y spikes 4.70%+, DXY 101+, equities extend the selloff — $SPY PUT / $VIX CALL setups. $XLE and $GLD squeeze. Fed hike risk re-prices higher into the July FOMC; higher-for-longer hardens.
scenarios.invalidation: Hot print driven by a one-off fuel-surcharge/transportation component while core goods deflate and oil fades.

scenarios.label: Cooler disinflation impulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline 0.0% to +0.1% m/m, Core +0.15% m/m — surprise Hormuz de-escalation/reopening drops oil, shelter decelerates faster, services ex-shelter soft; y/y dips toward 3.4%.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y drops to 4.40-4.45%, DXY <100. Risk-on relief: $QQQ +1-2%, $TLT strong, homebuilders ($DHI) and REITs rally, airlines ($UAL) bounce on cheaper jet fuel. Fed-cut odds for late 2026 revive.
scenarios.invalidation: The Hormuz escalation dominates regardless, or the market already priced a benign print.

setups.bias: Tactical long — July Hormuz escalation + blockade reinstatement
setups.trigger: WTI holds above $78 and Hormuz traffic stays disrupted into late July
setups.invalidation: Surprise de-escalation/reopening sends WTI back below $70.

setups.bias: Long — July energy/inflation tail + geopolitics hedge
setups.trigger: DXY below 101 and real yields compress; escalation persists
setups.invalidation: DXY above 101.5 with 10Y above 4.75% kills the haven bid.

setups.bias: Light short / avoid longs into a firming July print
setups.trigger: 10Y fails 4.45% and core CPI comes in cool
setups.invalidation: 10Y breaks 4.70% on hot July CPI or Hormuz-driven inflation repricing.

setups.bias: Fade strength — higher-for-longer + chip capex concerns
setups.trigger: Bounce into resistance with VIX below 18 and firm July CPI
setups.invalidation: Cool July CPI + de-escalation revives rate-cut pricing.

setups.bias: Avoid / light short — jet fuel spike risk on the July blockade
setups.trigger: WTI above $80 with summer demand softening
setups.invalidation: De-escalation drops jet fuel and bookings hold.

setups.bias: Protective — hot July CPI + FOMC + geopolitics = vol expansion
setups.trigger: Core >= +0.3% or Hormuz escalation; VIX breakout above 20
setups.invalidation: Cool CPI + de-escalation crushes vol back below 15.

watchItems.label: MEASURED MONTH
watchItems.note: This snapshot measures JULY 2026 price changes. Publication timing (Aug 12) does not change the measured month. Prior print shown = May actual; June actual releases today Jul 14 08:30 ET and becomes the new prior.

watchItems.label: Hormuz / Iran (JULY DRIVER)
watchItems.note: PROVISIONAL. Jul 7: Iran attacked 3 commercial vessels. Jul 8: US strikes, Trump declared ceasefire over, Treasury revoked Iran oil sanctions waiver (supply-side hit). Jul 13: blockade reinstated. This is a JULY measurement-window event that pushes July energy CPI up.

watchItems.label: AAA Retail Gasoline
watchItems.note: PROVISIONAL (July ~half done). Up from $3.797 a week ago; rising in the back half of July on the blockade. EIA API unavailable; AAA is primary source. WTI NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R2=0.23). EIA STEO: H2-2026 avg ~$3.60/gal.

watchItems.label: WTI Crude
watchItems.note: PROVISIONAL. Spiked from ~$66 early-July to ~$79 on the escalation. Directional only for energy trades — NOT used for gasoline CPI forecast. Rest of July's path sets the energy print.

watchItems.label: Cleveland Fed CPI Nowcast
watchItems.note: Model cross-check for JULY 2026. Broadly consistent with our bottom-up (headline 3.7%, core 2.8%).

watchItems.label: Market-implied July CPI
watchItems.note: Kalshi/Robinhood July CPI y/y: >3.5% 84¢, >3.6% 45¢, >3.7% 35¢, >3.8% 19¢. Median ~3.6-3.7%. No formal economist consensus yet this early.

watchItems.label: 10Y UST / VIX / SPX
watchItems.note: Jul 13. 10Y regime line: <4.45% growth relief, >4.70% risk-off. VIX +14% pre-data. Nasdaq -2% on chip capex concerns — stretched positioning.

watchItems.label: Fed path / FOMC
watchItems.note: July FOMC lands BEFORE the July CPI (Aug 12). June FOMC was a hawkish hold, easing bias removed, dot plot 3.8% median = one 2026 hike. This print shapes September.

keyDrivers: MEASURED MONTH = JULY 2026. The Hormuz escalation is a JULY-window event (Jul 7-8-13), so July energy CPI turns back UP after June's deflation — this is the dominant July driver.

Energy re-acceleration: AAA gasoline rising in the back half of July on the blockade reinstatement (WTI ~$79). EIA API unavailable; AAA is primary source. WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R2=0.23, MAE=8.1%).
Core keeps cooling: shelter decelerating (~0.25% m/m, Zillow/Apartment List leading) but services ex-shelter sticky. July core lands ~+0.2% / 2.8% y/y.
PROVISIONAL / LOW confidence: July is only ~2 weeks in. The back-half oil path (escalation vs de-escalation) decides whether July prints firm (base) or hot. Confidence held at 50.
Supply-side oil risk: Treasury revoked Iran's oil export sanctions waiver (Jul 8) — a structural July supply hit, not just shipping risk.
Strong USD (~100) = core-goods import deflation, partially offsetting sticky services and energy.
FOMC timing: July FOMC (Jul 28-29) lands BEFORE this print (Aug 12), so July CPI mainly shapes the September decision.
risks: Hormuz escalation (top July risk): a wider US-Iran flare-up or sustained blockade spikes oil through July and pushes the print HOT (y/y back toward 4%).
PROVISIONAL data risk: only ~2 weeks of July inputs; the back-half oil path can swing the energy component materially in either direction.
Supply-side persistence: sanctions-waiver revocation (Jul 8) could keep oil elevated beyond a shipping headline, making the energy rise structural.
Sticky core services: a +0.3%+ core print accelerates higher-for-longer repricing and extends the equity selloff.
De-escalation whipsaw: a surprise reopening collapses oil and flips the print cool — the scenario cuts both ways.
Demand weakness: oil spike on supply fear + falling risk assets could read as stagflationary, not bullish.
PPI passthrough: watch the July PPI (Aug ~13) for pipeline confirmation into the July CPI core-goods read.
